**Subjective**
**Chief Complaint**
Acute onset left big toe pain, significant discomfort, unable to bear weight on left foot.

**History of Present Illness**
Mr. Delacroix presented with acute onset left big toe pain that woke him from sleep approximately twelve hours prior to the visit. The pain has been worsening since onset and he reports being unable to put any weight on the left foot. He was unable to tolerate a sheet touching the toe. He has a prior history of gout in the same toe about three years ago, treated at urgent care during that episode, but he never followed up and was never started on any urate-lowering therapy. He was only treated for the acute flare with diet modification at that time. No daily medications for gout since then.
